structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Fan Control – CWRT Control (Close Coupled)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Control tower fan speed to maintain CWRT at setpoint. The speed command is the lowest of CWRTTowerSpd, HpTowerMaxSpd from each chiller, and PlrTowerMaxSpd.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Any condenser water pump is proven on.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Maintain CWRT at setpoint using a direct acting PID loop. Map loop output to CWRTTowerSpd from minimum speed to 100%. Apply the lowest of CWRTTowerSpd, HpTowerMaxSpd, and PlrTowerMaxSpd as the speed command to all operating fans.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When any condenser water pump is proven on, CWRT shall be maintained at setpoint by a direct acting PID loop. The loop output shall be mapped to the variable CWRTTowerSpd. Map CWRTTowerSpd from minimum tower speed at 0% loop output to 100% speed at 100% loop output. Tower speed command signal shall be the lowest value of CWRTTowerSpd, HpTowerMaxSpd from each chiller head pressure control loop, and PlrTowerMaxSpd. All operating fans shall receive the same speed signal.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Fan Enable/Disable (CWRT Control)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Disable tower fans if any enabled chiller's HpTowerMaxSpd has been at minimum for 5 minutes, or if fans have been at minimum speed for 5 minutes and CWRT drops below setpoint minus 1°F. Enable fans if off for at least 1 minute, CWRT rises above setpoint by 1°F, and all enabled chillers' HpTowerMaxSpd are above minimum.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Disable: (any enabled chiller's HpTowerMaxSpd = minimum for 5 min) OR (fans at minimum for 5 min AND CWRT < setpoint - 1°F). Enable: off for ≥1 min AND CWRT > setpoint + 1°F AND all HpTowerMaxSpd > minimum.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Disable or enable tower fans as per conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Disable the tower fans if either i. Any enabled chiller’s HpTowerMaxSpd has equaled tower minimum speed for 5 minutes, or ii. Tower fans have been at minimum speed for 5 minutes and CWRT drops below setpoint minus 1°F. Enable the tower fans if i. They have been off for at least 1 minute, and ii. CWRT rises above setpoint by 1°F, and iii. All enabled chillers’ HpTowerMaxSpd are greater than tower minimum speed.

From 51-100%, if DAT > room temp + 3°C, reset airflow setpoint from heating minimum to heating maximum. Modulate heating coil to maintain DAT setpoint.

configurable_values: MaxΔT (maximum DAT rise above heating setpoint)

From 0% to 50%, the heating-loop output shall reset the discharge temperature setpoint from the current AHU SAT setpoint to a maximum of MaxΔT above space temperature setpoint. The active airflow setpoint shall be the heating minimum endpoint. b. From 51% to 100%, if the DAT is greater than room temperature plus 3°C (5°F), the heating-loop output shall reset the active airflow setpoint from the heating minimum endpoint to the heating maximum endpoint. c. The heating coil shall be modulated to maintain the discharge temperature at setpoint.

For a heating-loop signal of 50% to 0%, SATsp is reset from Heat_SAT to the active zone heating setpoint. 5. The heating coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Cooling coil OFF

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hierarchical Alarm Suppression
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Alarms from load components are suppressed if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system. Suppression cascades through multiple levels. Life/safety alarms, failure-to-start, and failure-to-stop alarms are never suppressed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The BAS shall selectively suppress (i.e., fail to announce; alarms may still be logged to a database) alarms for load components if SystemOK is FALSE for the source system that serves that load. ... This hierarchical suppression shall cascade through multiple levels of load-source relationship such that alarms at downstream loads shall also be suppressed. ... The following types of alarms will never be suppressed by this logic: a. Life/safety and Level 1 alarms b. Failure-to-start alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded ON, but status point shows equipment to be OFF) c. Failure-to-stop/hand alarms (i.e., equipment is commanded OFF, but status point shows equipment to be ON)

evidence_quote: g. The source,  per Section 5.1.19 , that serves th e equipment in alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Equipment Staging and Rotation – Lead/Standby
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Lead/standby equipment shall be alternated when more than one is off, based on Staging Runtime. If equipment runs continuously, positions switch at an adjustable day/time, with standby started before lead is shut off.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Lead/standby equipment: a. Unless equipment runs continuously, parallel equipment that are 100% redundant shall be lead/standby alternated when more than one of the equipment is off so that the equipment with the most operating hours as determined by Staging Runtime is made the last stage equipment and the one with the least number of hours is made the earlier stage equipment. b. If equipment runs continuously, lead/standby positions shall switch at an adjustable day of the week and time (e.g., every Tuesday at 10:00 am) based on Staging Runtime; standby equipment shall first be started and proven on before former lead equipment is changed to standby and shut off.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Faulted Equipment Handling – Fans, Pumps, Cooling Towers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon fault, the next commanded-off equipment is started while the alarming equipment remains on. If the replacement also fails, repeat until required quantity is met. Alarming equipment is moved to last in staging order.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon identification of a fault condition: i. For fans, pumps, and cooling towers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” remains commanded on. (b) If Equipment “B” fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), it shall remain commanded on and the preceding step shall be repeated until the quantity of equipment called for by the current stage has proven on, or there are no more available equipment. (c) Set alarming equipment to the last positions in the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order sequenced reverse chronologically (i.e., the equipment that alarmed most recently is sent to last position). (d) Staging order of non-alarming equipment shall follow the even wear logic. Equipment in alarm can only automatically move up on the staging order if another equipment goes into alarm. (e) Equipment in alarm shall run if so called for by the lead/lag or lead/standby staging order and present stage.

evidence_quote: 5.1.15.5.  Exceptions to Lead/lag and Lead/standby rotation
anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
evidence_text: 5.1.15.5.  Exceptions to Lead/lag and Lead/standby rotation
judge_reason: Incomplete evidence; faulted equipment handling for fans/pumps/towers unsupported.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Faulted Equipment Handling – Chillers and Boilers
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon fault, the alarming equipment is commanded off and the next equipment is commanded on. If the replacement fails, repeat until all equipment tried. If all fail, the most recently alarmed remains on.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon identification of a fault condition: ii. For chillers and boilers: (a) The next commanded off equipment in the staging order, Equipment “B”, shall be commanded on while alarming Equipment “A” is commanded off and set to the last position in the lead/lag staging order. (b) If Equipment B fails to prove status (i.e., it also goes into alarm), repeat the preceding step until the quantity of equipment called for by the lead/lag logic have proven on or until all equipment has been tried. (c) If all equipment has been tried and the quantity of non-alarming equipment is less than called for then the most recently alarmed equipment will remain commanded on.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Hand Operation Detection – Fans and Pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If status point does not match OFF command for 15 seconds after a 60-second delay, equipment is considered ON-in-hand. It is set to lead device and a Level 4 alarm is generated.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Hand Operation. If a piece of equipment is ON-in-hand (e.g., via an HOA switch or local control of VFD), the equipment shall be set to the lead device, and a Level 4 alarm shall be generated. The equipment will remain as lead until. Hand operation is determined by the following: 1. Fans and Pumps i. Status point not matching its ON/OFF point for 15 seconds after a time delay of 60 seconds when the equipment is commanded OFF.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV Box Controllable Minimum Determination
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Determine the lowest possible VAV box airflow setpoint (Vm) allowed by controls. Use manufacturer data for minimum velocity pressure signal (VPm). Calculate Vm using Option 1 (amplification factor) or Option 2 (rated operating point).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: This section is used to determine the lowest possible VAV box airflow setpoint (other than zero) allowed by the controls (Vm) used in VAV box control sequences. The minimums shall be stored as software points. a. First, determine the velocity pressure sensor reading VPm in Pa (in. of water) that will give a reliable flow indication using product literature from the manufacturer of the VAV box controller. If this information is not available from the controller manufacturer, assume 1% of the velocity pressure sensor’s differential pressure range. b. Next, determine minimum setpoint Vm using either of the following: 1. Option 1. Determine the minimum velocity vm for each VAV box size and model. ... 2. Option 2: Use airflow vs. signal pressure data published by the manufacturer of the VAV box velocity pressure probe.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CHW Plant Operating States for AFDD
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Operating states defined by valve positions and pump statuses: #1 Disabled, #2 One chiller enabled, #3 More than one chiller enabled, #4 Waterside economizer-only, #5 Integrated waterside economizer.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Operating State (OS) of the plant shall be defined by the commanded positions of the valves and status feedback from the pumps in accordance with the following table. Operating State Chiller CHW Isolation Valves (if Series Chillers) Chiller CHW Isolation Valves or Dedicated PCHWPs (if Parallel Chillers) Chiller CW Isolation Valves or Dedicated CWPs (if water-cooled) CHW Pump Status CW Pump Status (if water-cooled and headered) WSE CHW Pump Status (if WSE with HX Pump) WSE CHW Diverting Valve (if WSE with HX diverting valve) WSE CW Isolation Valve (if WSE) Chiller Bypass Valve (if primary-only or WSE) #1: Disabled All Open All Closed/Off All Closed/Off All Off All Off Off Open Closed Closed #2: One Chiller Enabled One Closed, All Others Open One Open/On, All Others Closed/Off One Modulating, All Others Closed/Off Any On Any On Off 100% Open Closed Closed #3: More than one Chiller Enabled Both Closed More than one Open/On More than one modulating Any On Any On Off 100% Open Closed Closed #4: Waterside Economizer-only All Open All Closed/Off All Closed/Off Any On Any On On < 100% Open Open Open #5: Integrated Waterside Economizer Any Open or Any Closed Any Open/On or Any Closed/Off Any Modulating or Any Closed/Off Any On Any On On < 100% Open Open Closed

structured_payload_candidate.title: HW Plant Operating States for AFDD
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Operating states defined by boiler isolation valve/dedicated pump status and primary or secondary pump status: #1 Disabled, #2 One boiler enabled, #3 More than one boiler enabled.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Operating State (OS) of the plant shall be defined by the commanded positions of the valves and status feedback from the pumps in accordance with the following table. Operating State Boiler Isolation Valve or Dedicated Primary HW Pump Status PHW Pump Status (if primary-only) or SHW Pump Status (if primary-secondary) #1: Disabled All Closed/Off All Off #2: One boiler enabled One Open/On, All Others Closed/Off Any On #3: More than one boiler enabled Any Open/On Any On
